ads.author: Tommy Myers
ads.title: Vintage 1982 Atari Dig Dug Stand-Up Arcade Cabinet – Fully Working & Ready to Blast!
ads.description: Step into the golden age of arcades with this authentic 1982 Atari Dig Dug upright cabinet! This is the real deal — not a mini, not a replica, not a conversion. It’s a genuine vintage stand-up machine straight from the era when arcades ruled. Dig Dug is pure addictive fun: burrow through colorful underground tunnels, pump up the Pookas and Fygars until they explode, or drop rocks on them for massive points. Crisp graphics, thumping original sounds, responsive joystick and buttons — it plays exactly like you remember from the 80s! Condition: In great working condition with no issues. Everything fires up perfectly — screen is bright and clear, controls are tight, sound is loud and spot-on. No flickering, no dead spots, no repairs needed. It’s been well-maintained and is ready to provide endless entertainment right out of the box. This cabinet is a show-stopper for any: • Game room • Man cave • Basement bar • Retro collector’s lineup These original dedicated 1982 uprights are getting rarer every year, especially in solid working shape like this one. Price: $1,900 or best offer Local pickup only (cash preferred). It’s a big, heavy classic (roughly 72" tall), so bring a friend or a truck — happy to help load it! Serious buyers only — message me anytime with questions or to set up a time to come test it out and play a few rounds. First come, first served. #DigDug #VintageArcade #Atari1982 #RetroGaming #ArcadeCabinet #ClassicArcade

ads.description: I’m selling a super nice Atari kangaroo arcade game. This machine is in great shape and plays well. The artwork is still bright and vibrant and it’s still running the original CRT monitor. If you have any questions, please message me.

ads.author: Benjamin Cook
ads.title: 1996 Sega Wave Runner Arcade Machine
ads.description: *If it's up, it's available. Local pickup near Boiling Springs SC. Electronic payments (Cash app, venmo, zelle etc) are not accepted. Cash only please* I had planned on restoring this arcade machine but life got in the way. It powers on and works to an extent but it needs TLC to get it fully functional. The picture convergence needs help. The jet ski needs an air compressor to make the ride functional which is missing so I never could test it. There's also a piece of aluminum trim on the left side of the jet ski that was missing when I got it. The Sega Model 2 inside has been replaced so the old one is included. You will need to bring a trailer with a ramp to load it. Both pieces are on wheels so it's relatively easy to move but very heavy.

I’m selling a handful of other arcade games as well, so if you’re building a home arcade or man cave, feel free to reach out. I’d be willing to make a great deal for someone who buys more than one of them at once.

Gottlieb Free Fall Pinball Machine (1974) The Gottlieb Free Fall is a classic single-player wedge head electro-mechanical pinball machine with a fun, family-friendly theme and timeless gameplay. Built during Gottlieb’s golden era, this game offers straightforward rules, smooth shooting, and that unmistakable EM charm. Highlights Single-player wedge head electro-mechanical pinball Fun, approachable theme that’s great for all ages Classic drop targets and lively pop bumpers Clean, colorful playfield artwork New rubber rings installed New lights for bright, crisp playfield illumination It comes with a 6 month warranty for local buyer

ads.author: Jon Sutcliffe
ads.title: Centuri Gyruss Arcade Game
ads.description: This is an original working 1983 Centuri Gyruss arcade game with a 19" CRT. 100% working - No issues - All works as it should This was in an arcade for some of its 43 year history. It's not perfect but pretty nice and very clean. Look at video and pictures, ask for more. If you're local, come to look it over and play a few games! Owner's manual and schematics are in backdoor pocket. Keys and locks for coin door and backdoor. Set to free play but can be configured to accept quarters or tokens. (Quarter coin mechs are included) All Original: - Gryuss PCBs and wiring harnesses - G07-CBO 19"CRT and chassis - Marquee and Bezel - Side art - Joystick New: - Control Panel Overlay art - Buttons - Message me to discuss game details, ask for more pictures, videos. - Prefer local pickup so you can see it and play it. I will help load. - Will ship if you make all shipper arrangements - Can deliver ~locally to your door for a fee. No stairs or other adventures. Make me an offer! Cash or PayPal. No Checks. I will consider trade for a pinball.

ads.author: Chris Burant
ads.title: Phoenix Pinball machine
ads.description: This Williams Phoenix from 1978 is a four player early solid state pin ball machine. Player's condition. Every switch, button, light, and function has been gone through meticulously by a seasoned technician. And issues 100% functioning. The cabinet shows wear, but still sports the original pink paint. Yhe backglass has some thin spots and the playfield shows its age in areas around the inserts. The drop targets have been rebuilt, all new rubbers and lights installed. Refreshed power supply and sound boards. New MPU/ Driver combo (original boards available by request). Installed extra fuses to mitigate fire risk. This pin will be shown and available to play at Louisville Arcade Expo from 3/27 - 3/29. You can come try it at my home prior to then as well. Reach out if you would like alternate photos or have any questions. Open to trades, and local delivery and setup for a fee. Pickup at Expo would be discounted.

ads.author: Tim Timmins
ads.title: Dig Dug Video Arcade Game Machine ORIGINAL WORKING Atari Namco
ads.description: Here's an original Dig Dug arcade game - this is the REAL DEAL from back in the day! The game is running all original hardware and works fully, passing all self-tests and retaining all scores when turned off as it should. All buttons, switches, lights (yes, even the coin door lights) work. The cabinet has had wear around the edges and one area that was needed bondo as well. These areas were sanded, sealed and repainted. The t-molding was removed for this process, which was then cleaned and reinstalled. The black player-facing areas were all repainted and look perfect. The original sideart is all present and is in very good shape with no major gouges or defects. The marquee is original and in good shape. The original glass bezel artwork was stripped (flaking badly) and new repro reverse-printed artwork applied - looks a very nice-a. The control panel overlay is original and in very good shape, with only one small, tasteful cigarette burn in the upper right and some scuffing on the front. The panel itself was stripped of parts and the overlay cleaned with Awesome! and Magic Eraser to make those pinks and yellows really pop! The buttons and joystick were disassembled and cleaned. The bolts holding the joystick were also removed and burnished. The original coin door was disassembled, stripped and refinished and looks perfect! It can take quarters if you wish, as the coin box is present, but no locks are included. The original monitor has had the voltage properly adjusted and looks very good. Best thing is - IT'S BURN-FREE, a rarity for this title. Lastly, the original back door is included, with the instruction sheet AND the manual and schematics package. This is one of my favs - make it yours today! Cash and carry in Plano. Play before you pay. First floor, no stairs and I can help you load! :D

It's most likely a switch contact somewhere that's broken, but I've been too busy to diagnose it, and I need the room, so I'm willing to sell it for less than I paid to someone who wants a unique game, and is willing to tinker with it. This game is basically in original condition except for being upgraded with $200+ worth of LEDs to make it brighter and more reliable.

Joust, very clean - $1500 Multi-Williams, good shape - $1200 Gauntlet Legends, monitor may need work - $700 Virtua Fighter - $800 Red Neo Geo 4-Slot - $800 STUN Runner - $700 60-in-1 Cocktail, needs new top glass - $500 Ms. Pacman, works, cab in rough shape - $500 Soul Calibur conversion cab, needs work - $300 Pictures here: https://postimg.cc/user/doubletap
